$comment: Memory gardening config — governs /skill:gardening passes only. Has zero effect on interactive work, normal Pi sessions, commits, or any other skill.
$harnessRoot: Resolve to ~/.pi (os.homedir()/.pi) at load time. All denied paths expand against this root once into absolute form. The deny-list is anchored to the harness, never cwd — works identically whether gardening runs from ~/.pi, a project directory, or anywhere else.

budgets.$comment: Token budgets for memory files. observeMode=true means budgets are measured but not enforced — compression skips until observeMode is false.

decay.$comment: Lesson decay parameters. citedReset: decay resets to this on citation. ignoredFactor: multiplier per uncited session. candidateBelow: demote candidate threshold. graceSessions: new lessons immune to demotion for this many sessions.

anchors.$comment: Anchor lessons are damage-preventing — they get extra scrutiny before modification.

sweeps.$comment: Max age before sweep passes archive or delete files.

paths.$comment: Bidirectional gate — applies ONLY to gardening's automated passes. Never touches interactive work.
paths.$harnessRoot_note: Set to resolve(os.homedir(), '.pi') at load time. All denied paths below are resolved against this root into absolute paths before any matching. This ensures the deny-list works identically whether gardening is running from ~/.pi, a project directory, or anywhere else on the filesystem.

denied.$comment:
Protective (out): gardening must never delete or move files under these paths. Resolve BOTH sides to canonical absolute form — expand these prefixes against harnessRoot at load, resolve every fs target to absolute before comparing. Absolute-vs-absolute, always, no matter what cwd is.

Worked example: cwd = C:\Users\Kasim Alam\Projects\DriftScout, harnessRoot = C:\Users\Kasim Alam\.pi, denied prefix = agent/skills/ → expanded to C:\Users\Kasim Alam\.pi\agent\skills\. Target C:\Users\Kasim Alam\.pi\agent\skills\skill-grill.md resolves to ...\agent\skills\skill-grill.md. startsWith(C:\Users\Kasim Alam\.pi\agent\skills\) = true → BLOCKED. Matches correctly from any cwd.
denied.rule: Deny beats allow. At load: expand every denied path against harnessRoot to an absolute prefix. Before any fs write, move, or delete: path.resolve() the target to an absolute canonical path, then check startsWith against each expanded denied prefix. If any match, abort the operation and report. Apply this check in every automated pass: intake, merge, demote, compress, progress horizon, and all sweep passes. Pass 7 (break-in review) is exempt from this check because its edits are advisory-only and always human-applied — its targets are skill files on the deny-list by design. Pass 8 (report) writes only to allowed directories, checked separately.

allowed.$comment:
Preventive (in): gardening's transient artifacts (temp files, scratch work, generated reports) must land under these directories or scratchDir. Never the harness root or agent/.

ANCHORING ASYMMETRY (intentional): Denied paths resolve against harnessRoot (absolute, house-anchored) because they protect the shared harness core. Allowed paths are project-cwd-relative — when gardening runs in DriftScout, scratch lands in DriftScout/.agent/, not the harness's. Protection guards the shared house; scratch belongs to the room being cleaned.

NOTE: this prevents gardening from spawning junk, but does NOT prevent you from cd-ing into the wrong directory and running commands there. That half is covered by .gitignore hygiene (Phase 0a/4.5).
allowed.rule: Symmetric to the deny check. Before any create or write of a transient artifact (report, temp file, scratch work): path.resolve() the target against the project cwd, then verify it starts with a resolved allowed directory or scratchDir prefix. If it does not, redirect the write to scratchDir/. This makes the 'keep junk out of the repo' half hard — same enforcement as the deny-list, opposite direction.

autonomy.$comment: Pass autonomy levels. 'auto' = runs without confirmation. 'gated' = presents changes and waits for per-pass y/n. Never batch gate confirmations — one pass, one decision. Pass 7 (break-in) is always advisory-only (targets are on deny-list).

graduation.$comment: After this many clean gated runs with no rejections, a pass can be promoted from gated to auto.
